content:
Do you know how sometimes you get sick and your doctor gives you special medicine called antibiotics? Those medicines are like superheroes that help kill the bad guys, called germs, that make you feel sick. But sometimes, those bad guys can be very sneaky and find ways to avoid getting caught by the superheroes. When that happens, the medicine doesn't work as well, and that's called antibiotic resistance.

It's happening because we've been using antibiotics too much, and the bad guys have had time to figure out how to outsmart them. It's like they're wearing a special cloak that makes them invisible to the medicine. When we take antibiotics when we don't really need them, or don't finish the whole dose, it helps the bad guys get stronger and more resistant. That's why it's so important to only take antibiotics when we really need them, and to always finish the whole dose.
